mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the unemployment rate below 5.5% signals tight labor market conditions that support corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE < 5.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below -0.2 in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 105.0 signals strengthening manufacturing activity that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO > 105.0, or the predictive power of INDPRO declines to Rank IC < 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand that drives equity market optimism, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of HOUST and RSAFS fails to generate positive 60d returns in > 40% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations that depress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns exceeds 0.15 in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in payroll growth (PAYEMS) below 100k signals weakening labor demand that reduces corporate revenue visibility, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS < 100k, or the predictive power of PAYEMS declines to Rank IC < 0.02 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2.0% annually signals economic weakness that depresses equity market sentiment, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of instances where GDPC1 < 2.0%, or the correlation between GDPC1 and SPX returns falls below -0.2 in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising industrial production (INDPRO) and falling capacity utilization signals overcapacity that pressures profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and CAPUTIL fails to generate negative 20d returns in > 40% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) in regression models.
